title: Central European Summer Time

infobox.value: [Western European Time](/wiki/Western_European_Time "Western European Time") / [Greenwich Mean Time](/wiki/Greenwich_Mean_Time "Greenwich Mean Time") ([UTC](/wiki/UTC%2B00:00 "UTC+00:00"))
[Western European Time](/wiki/Western_European_Time "Western European Time") / [Greenwich Mean Time](/wiki/Greenwich_Mean_Time "Greenwich Mean Time") ([UTC](/wiki/UTC%2B00:00 "UTC+00:00"))

infobox.key: [Western European Summer Time](/wiki/Western_European_Summer_Time "Western European Summer Time") / [British Summer Time](/wiki/British_Summer_Time "British Summer Time") / [Irish Standard Time](/wiki/Time_in_the_Republic_of_Ireland "Time in the Republic of Ireland") ([UTC+1](/wiki/UTC%2B01:00 "UTC+01:00"))

infobox.value: | Yellow | [Eastern European Time](/wiki/Eastern_European_Time "Eastern European Time") / [Kaliningrad Time](/wiki/Kaliningrad_Time "Kaliningrad Time") ([UTC+2](/wiki/UTC%2B02:00 "UTC+02:00"))

infobox.key: [Eastern European Summer Time](/wiki/Eastern_European_Summer_Time "Eastern European Summer Time") ([UTC+3](/wiki/UTC%2B03:00 "UTC+03:00"))
infobox.value: | Green | [Moscow Time](/wiki/Moscow_Time "Moscow Time") / [Turkey Time](/wiki/Time_in_Turkey "Time in Turkey") ([UTC+3](/wiki/UTC%2B03:00 "UTC+03:00"))

[Armenia Time](/wiki/Armenia_Time "Armenia Time") / [Azerbaijan Time](/wiki/Azerbaijan_Time "Azerbaijan Time") / [Georgia Time](/wiki/Georgia_Time "Georgia Time") / [Samara Time](/wiki/Samara_Time "Samara Time") ([UTC+4](/wiki/UTC%2B04:00 "UTC+04:00"))

(**CEST**, [UTC+02:00](/wiki/UTC%2B02:00 "UTC+02:00")), sometimes referred to as **Central European Daylight Time** (**CEDT**),[[1]](#cite_note-1) is the standard clock time observed during the period of [summer daylight-saving](/wiki/Daylight_saving_time "Daylight saving time") in those European countries which observe [Central European Time](/wiki/Central_European_Time "Central European Time") (CET; [UTC+01:00](/wiki/UTC%2B01:00 "UTC+01:00")) during the other part of the year. It corresponds to [UTC+02:00](/wiki/UTC%2B02:00 "UTC+02:00"), which makes it the same as [Eastern European Time](/wiki/Eastern_European_Time "Eastern European Time"), [Central Africa Time](/wiki/Central_Africa_Time "Central Africa Time"), [South African Standard Time](/wiki/South_African_Standard_Time "South African Standard Time"), [Egypt Standard Time](/wiki/Egypt_Standard_Time "Egypt Standard Time") and [Kaliningrad Time](/wiki/Kaliningrad_Time "Kaliningrad Time") in [Russia](/wiki/Russia "Russia").[[2]](#cite_note-2)

["Time zone names- Middle European Daylight, Middle European Summer, Mitteieuropaische Sommerzeit (german)"](https://www.worldtimezone.com/wtz-names/wtz-med.html). *www.worldtimezone.com*. Retrieved 2018-07-20.
["CEDT - Central European Daylight Time: Current local time"](https://www.zeitverschiebung.net/en/abbr/36). *Time Difference*. Retrieved 2018-07-20.
Joseph Myers (2009-07-17). ["History of legal time in Britain"](http://www.srcf.ucam.org/~jsm28/british-time/). Retrieved 2009-10-11.

Boffey, Daniel (26 March 2019). ["European parliament votes to scrap daylight saving time from 2021"](https://www.theguardian.com/world/2019/mar/26/european-parliament-votes-to-scrap-daylight-saving-time-from-2021). *The Guardian*.
["CEST – Central European Summer Time (Time Zone Abbreviation)"](https://www.timeanddate.com/time/zones/cest). *www.timeanddate.com*. Retrieved 2018-07-20.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Daylight saving time in the Central European time zone

| Current time | |
| 19:25, 26 March 2026 [CET](/wiki/Central_European_Time "Central European Time") [[refresh](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Central_European_Summer_Time&action=purge)] | |

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/8/8e/Time_zones_of_the_Greater_Europe.svg/330px-Time_zones_of_the_Greater_Europe.svg.png)](/wiki/File:Time_zones_of_the_Greater_Europe.svg)

[Time in Europe](/wiki/Time_in_Europe "Time in Europe"):

|  |  |

| Light Blue | [Western European Time](/wiki/Western_European_Time "Western European Time") / [Greenwich Mean Time](/wiki/Greenwich_Mean_Time "Greenwich Mean Time") ([UTC](/wiki/UTC%2B00:00 "UTC+00:00")) |
| Blue | [Western European Time](/wiki/Western_European_Time "Western European Time") / [Greenwich Mean Time](/wiki/Greenwich_Mean_Time "Greenwich Mean Time") ([UTC](/wiki/UTC%2B00:00 "UTC+00:00")) |
| [Western European Summer Time](/wiki/Western_European_Summer_Time "Western European Summer Time") / [British Summer Time](/wiki/British_Summer_Time "British Summer Time") / [Irish Standard Time](/wiki/Time_in_the_Republic_of_Ireland "Time in the Republic of Ireland") ([UTC+1](/wiki/UTC%2B01:00 "UTC+01:00")) |

| [Eastern European Summer Time](/wiki/Eastern_European_Summer_Time "Eastern European Summer Time") ([UTC+3](/wiki/UTC%2B03:00 "UTC+03:00")) |

| Turquoise | [Armenia Time](/wiki/Armenia_Time "Armenia Time") / [Azerbaijan Time](/wiki/Azerbaijan_Time "Azerbaijan Time") / [Georgia Time](/wiki/Georgia_Time "Georgia Time") / [Samara Time](/wiki/Samara_Time "Samara Time") ([UTC+4](/wiki/UTC%2B04:00 "UTC+04:00")) |

▉▉▉▉ Pale hues: Standard time observed all year   
   ▉▉▉   Dark hues: [Daylight saving time](/wiki/Daylight_saving_time "Daylight saving time")

**Central European Summer Time** (**CEST**, [UTC+02:00](/wiki/UTC%2B02:00 "UTC+02:00")), sometimes referred to as **Central European Daylight Time** (**CEDT**),[[1]](#cite_note-1) is the standard clock time observed during the period of [summer daylight-saving](/wiki/Daylight_saving_time "Daylight saving time") in those European countries which observe [Central European Time](/wiki/Central_European_Time "Central European Time") (CET; [UTC+01:00](/wiki/UTC%2B01:00 "UTC+01:00")) during the other part of the year. It corresponds to [UTC+02:00](/wiki/UTC%2B02:00 "UTC+02:00"), which makes it the same as [Eastern European Time](/wiki/Eastern_European_Time "Eastern European Time"), [Central Africa Time](/wiki/Central_Africa_Time "Central Africa Time"), [South African Standard Time](/wiki/South_African_Standard_Time "South African Standard Time"), [Egypt Standard Time](/wiki/Egypt_Standard_Time "Egypt Standard Time") and [Kaliningrad Time](/wiki/Kaliningrad_Time "Kaliningrad Time") in [Russia](/wiki/Russia "Russia").[[2]](#cite_note-2)

## Names

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Central_European_Summer_Time&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Names")]

Other names which have been applied to Central European Summer Time are Middle European Summer Time (MEST),[[3]](#cite_note-3) Central European Daylight Saving Time (CEDT),[[4]](#cite_note-4) and Bravo Time (after the second letter of the [NATO phonetic alphabet](/wiki/NATO_phonetic_alphabet "NATO phonetic alphabet")).[[5]](#cite_note-5)

## Period of observation

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Central_European_Summer_Time&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Period of observation")]

Since 1996, [European Summer Time](/wiki/Summer_Time_in_Europe "Summer Time in Europe") has been observed between 01:00 UTC (02:00 CET and 03:00 CEST) on the last Sunday of March, and 01:00 UTC on the last Sunday of October; previously the rules were not uniform across the European Union.[[6]](#cite_note-Myers-6)

There were proposals to [abandon summer time in Europe](/wiki/Summer_Time_in_Europe#Future "Summer Time in Europe") from 2021, possibly by moving winter time up by an hour and keeping that time through the year.[[7]](#cite_note-7)

## Usage

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Central_European_Summer_Time&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Usage")]

The following countries and territories regularly use Central European Summer Time:[[8]](#cite_note-8)

* [Albania](/wiki/Albania "Albania"), since 1974
* [Andorra](/wiki/Andorra "Andorra"), since 1985
* [Austria](/wiki/Austria "Austria"), since 1980
* [Belgium](/wiki/Belgium "Belgium"), since 1980
* [Bosnia and Herzegovina](/wiki/Bosnia_and_Herzegovina "Bosnia and Herzegovina"), since 1945 when part of [Yugoslavia](/wiki/Socialist_Federal_Republic_of_Yugoslavia "Socialist Federal Republic of Yugoslavia")
* [Croatia](/wiki/Croatia "Croatia"), since 1945 when part of Yugoslavia
* [Czech Republic](/wiki/Czech_Republic "Czech Republic"), since 1979 when part of [Czechoslovakia](/wiki/Czechoslovakia "Czechoslovakia")
* [Denmark](/wiki/Denmark "Denmark") (metropolitan), since 1980
* [France](/wiki/Metropolitan_France "Metropolitan France") (metropolitan), since 1976
* [Germany](/wiki/Daylight_saving_time_in_Germany "Daylight saving time in Germany"), since 1980
* [Gibraltar](/wiki/Gibraltar "Gibraltar"), since 1982
* [Hungary](/wiki/Hungary "Hungary"), since 1980
* [Italy](/wiki/Italy "Italy"), since 1968
* [Kosovo](/wiki/Kosovo "Kosovo"), since 1945 when part of Yugoslavia
* [Liechtenstein](/wiki/Liechtenstein "Liechtenstein"), since 1981
* [Luxembourg](/wiki/Luxembourg "Luxembourg"), since 1981
* [Malta](/wiki/Malta "Malta"), since 1974
* [Monaco](/wiki/Monaco "Monaco"), since 1976
* [Montenegro](/wiki/Montenegro "Montenegro"), since 1945 when part of Yugoslavia
* [Netherlands](/wiki/Netherlands "Netherlands"), since 1977
* [North Macedonia](/wiki/North_Macedonia "North Macedonia"), since 1945 when part of Yugoslavia
* [Norway](/wiki/Norway "Norway"), since 1980
* [Poland](/wiki/Poland "Poland"), since 1977
* [San Marino](/wiki/San_Marino "San Marino"), since 1966
* [Serbia](/wiki/Serbia "Serbia"), since 1945 when part of Yugoslavia
* [Slovakia](/wiki/Slovakia "Slovakia"), since 1979 when part of Czechoslovakia
* [Slovenia](/wiki/Slovenia "Slovenia"), since 1983 when part of Yugoslavia
* [Spain](/wiki/Spain "Spain"), since 1974 (except [Canary Islands](/wiki/Canary_Islands "Canary Islands"), which instead apply [Western European Summer Time](/wiki/Western_European_Summer_Time "Western European Summer Time"))
* [Sweden](/wiki/Sweden "Sweden"), since 1980
* [Switzerland](/wiki/Switzerland "Switzerland"), since 1981
* [Vatican City](/wiki/Vatican_City "Vatican City"), since 1966

The following countries have also used Central European Summer Time in the past:

* [Libya](/wiki/Libya "Libya"), 1951–1959, 1982–1989, 1996–1997, 2012–2013
* [Lithuania](/wiki/Lithuania "Lithuania"), 1998–1999
* [Portugal](/wiki/Portugal "Portugal"), 1993–1995
* [Tunisia](/wiki/Tunisia "Tunisia"), 2005–2008
